key_insight: DFT can miss the true local stacking energetics of bilayer InSe: registries with the same interfacial Se motif are not actually nearly degenerate once many-body effects are included.
engineering_relevance: Moiré and relaxation models built from DFT may underestimate stacking-energy corrugation, leading to incorrect predictions of domain formation, structural relaxation, and electronic reconstruction in twisted layered materials.
method: Benchmarked bilayer InSe local-registry energetics with diffusion quantum Monte Carlo (DMC).
Compared DMC results against DFT predictions for AB, AAr, and ABr stackings.
Analyzed how charge-density response and full registry dependence affect stacking stability.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that DFT places AB, AAr, and ABr within 1.5 meV/f.u., whereas DMC separates them by 8(5) and 41(4) meV/f.u., with a 60(7) meV/f.u. spread between the most and least stable registries.
watch_item: These results are specific to bilayer InSe, so extending the conclusion to other materials or twist angles may require separate many-body benchmarking.
paper_title: Many-body benchmarking of DFT local-registry energetics in bilayer InSe
